channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Tanzania explores coal resources to strengthen energy security and industrial growth
item.description: Coal in Tanzania is being highlighted as an important natural resource that is still not fully used, but with strong potential to support the country’s energy security and economic development. The de...

item.title: UN resolution on transatlantic slave trade and its push for global historical justice and reparations
item.description: The article discusses a recent United Nations General Assembly resolution that formally recognises the transatlantic slave trade as the gravest crime against humanity. The resolution, passed with supp...

item.title: Ex-student opens fire at high school in Turkiye, 16 injured, attacker dies
item.description: On Tuesday, a tragic incident occurred at a high school in Sanliurfa province, Turkiye, where a 19-year-old former student opened fire at his former school. The attack left 16 people injured, most of ...

item.title: Kiambu Family Builds Four-Bedroom Home for Mum After Losing Previous House
item.description: A family in Kiambu County has touched many hearts after building their mother a new four-bedroom house worth KSh 9.5 million, following a painful period in which she lost her previous home to people s...

item.title: Zari Hassan calmly ignores admirer during Shakib’s boxing match appearance
item.description: Zari Hassan has gone viral after a moment at a boxing event involving her husband, Shakib, where she was seen calmly ignoring a man who appeared openly admiring her. The incident happened during a mat...

item.title: New Study Finds Genetics Account for Half of Human Lifespan Differences
item.description: A new study from the Weizmann Institute of Science challenges decades of assumptions about human longevity, revealing that genetics may play a far larger role in determining lifespan than previously b...

item.title: Claims and counterclaims surround US airman rescue operation in Iran amid speculation over alternative objectives
item.description: A widely discussed report examines the controversy surrounding a reported US military operation in Iran following the downing of an F-15 fighter jet. According to official accounts cited in political ...

item.title: Bafana Face Balanced but Testing Group A at 2026 World Cup
item.description: The draw for Group A of the 2026 FIFA World Cup appears evenly balanced, with South Africa’s Bafana Bafana up against Mexico, Czechia, and South Korea — three sides with solid pedigree and proven tour...

item.title: Mark Carney Achieves Parliamentary Majority in Canadian Special Elections
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney has secured a majority in Parliament for his Liberal government following special elections in Ontario. This outcome strengthens Carney’s position, allowing him to ...

item.title: Analysis of U.S. global influence decline and NATO fragmentation amid geopolitical shifts
item.description: The article presents a critical geopolitical commentary based on a conversation with retired U.S. Colonel Lawrence Wilkerson. It argues that the current international order is destabilizing rather tha...

item.title: Actress Nasiphi Ntabeni Reflects on Journey to Success in 'Inimba'
item.description: Actress Nasiphi Ntabeni, currently captivating audiences with her role as Ntsiki Mabandla in the popular telenovela *Inimba*, has revealed a remarkable journey of growth and self-discovery within the ...

item.title: Neville warns Arsenal to stay focused ahead of crucial Premier League clash with Manchester City
item.description: Former Manchester United defender Gary Neville has urged Arsenal to remain fully focused as they prepare for a decisive Premier League clash against Manchester City at the Etihad Stadium. According to...

item.title: Ex-Nato Chief Warns UK Defence Underfunding Poses National Security Risk
item.description: George Robertson, former Nato secretary general and ex-defence secretary, has sharply criticised the UK government for a 'corrosive complacency' towards defence, warning it endangers national security...

item.title: Government services continue after Mthatha Botha Sigcau building fire, says Eastern Cape Premier
item.description: Government services in the Eastern Cape are continuing despite the severe fire that damaged the iconic Botha Sigcau building in Mthatha on 24 March 2026. The building, once the headquarters of the for...

item.title: DCEA intensifies anti-drug crackdown, seizes nearly two tonnes of narcotics across Tanzania
item.description: The Drug Control and Enforcement Authority (DCEA) of Tanzania has seized almost two tonnes of illegal drugs and arrested 77 suspects following a nationwide crackdown in March. Commissioner General Are...

item.title: MMDA prepares measures for expected 3-day transport strike affecting commuters
item.description: The Metropolitan Manila Development Authority (MMDA) has announced its preparedness for an anticipated three-day transport strike that is expected to affect commuters in Metro Manila. In response to t...

item.title: Kochi Biennale Showcases Powerful Art Inspired by Ambedkar and Anti-Caste Movements
item.description: The Kochi-Muziris Biennale recently concluded, leaving behind a poignant display of artwork deeply rooted in Ambedkarite philosophy and anti-caste movements. The exhibit highlighted the enduring strug...

item.title: Elon Musk disputes South Africa Starlink licence denial over B-BBEE ownership requirements
item.description: Elon Musk don publicly accuse South African government of discrimination after his satellite internet company, Starlink, no fit secure operating licence for the country. According to Musk, the refusal...

item.title: Trump’s Conflict with the Pope Sparks Backlash Among Religious Supporters
item.description: Donald Trump has ignited a new controversy with the Catholic Church by publicly attacking Pope Leo XIV, the first American Pope, who criticized the U.S. war in Iran. Trump labelled the Pope as 'weak o...

item.title: Twelve police recruits in Nairobi charged over alleged KCSE certificate forgery
item.description: Twelve police recruits in Nairobi have been charged in court over allegations of forging Kenya Certificate of Secondary Education (KCSE) documents in an attempt to secure jobs in the Kenya Police Serv...
